title: $G^0W^0$ implementation based on the pseudopotential and numerical-atomic-orbital basis-set framework: Algorithms and benchmarks
authors: Huanjing Gong, Min-Ye Zhang, Peize Lin, Bohan Jia, Ziqing Guan, Lixin He, Xinguo Ren
abstract: The $GW$ method delivers substantially improved accuracy in electronic band structure calculations over conventional Kohn-Sham density functional theory (KS-DFT) by explicitly incorporating the electron self-energy effect beyond mean-field approximations. Despite many existing implementations, a periodic $GW$ implementation within the framework of numerical atomic orbitals (NAO) combined with the pseudopotential (PP) scheme has not been reported. This is urgently needed given the increasing popularity of the NAO-PP framework in KS-DFT calculations and its importance for the development of machine-learning electronic-structure approaches. In this work, we present an efficient NAO-PP-based $G^0W^0$ computational framework by interfacing the first-principles software package ABACUS with LibRPA -- a library for performing low-scaling random-phase approximation and $GW$ calculations based on NAOs. Our approach employs the localized resolution of identity (LRI) technique with a novel compression scheme, significantly improving both computational efficiency and numerical stability. In addition, an analytic treatment of the small-q limit of the microscopic dielectric function reduces the need for dense q-point sampling. Furthermore, we propose a practical strategy to select a suitable KS-DFT pseudopotential prior to $G^0W^0$ calculations by examining the frequency-dependent macroscopic dielectric function. Systematic benchmarks validate the effectiveness of our compression scheme and real-space tensor filtering strategies, demonstrating both high accuracy and significant computational efficiency gains. Comparisons with established $G^0W^0$ implementations show excellent agreement in band structures and band gaps, confirming ABACUS+LibRPA as a reliable and efficient platform for large-scale $G^0W^0$ simulations.
summary: This paper presents a new, efficient computational framework for performing G0W0 calculations using numerical atomic orbitals and pseudopotentials. By introducing a novel compression scheme and optimized integration techniques, the authors enable large-scale, accurate many-body perturbation theory simulations for periodic systems.
main_problem: The lack of an efficient, periodic G0W0 implementation within the Numerical Atomic Orbital (NAO) and pseudopotential (PP) framework, which is necessary for large-scale electronic structure calculations and machine-learning approaches.
main_result: The development of the ABACUS+LibRPA framework, which uses a novel LRI compression scheme and analytic q-point treatment to achieve high accuracy and significant computational efficiency gains, comparable to all-electron and plane-wave methods.
method: A space-time G0W0 algorithm using the Localized Resolution of Identity (LRI) technique, a novel compression scheme for auxiliary basis functions, and an analytic treatment of the small-q limit of the dielectric function.
model_or_system: Periodic crystalline solids (semiconductors and insulators like Si, GaAs, MgO, etc.) treated within the G0W0 approximation of many-body perturbation theory using a pseudopotential and NAO basis set.
key_observables: Quasiparticle energies, electronic band structures, band gaps, and the macroscopic dielectric function.
important_parameters: k-point and q-point sampling density, NAO cutoff radius, auxiliary basis function (ABF) size, and minimax integration grid points.
main_assumptions: The use of the frozen-core approximation and the reliance on the accuracy of the underlying KS-DFT pseudopotentials.
figures_summary: Figure 1 compares the macroscopic dielectric constant of GaAs across different pseudopotentials; Figure 2 shows GaAs quasiparticle band structures; Figure 7 displays convergence tests for Silicon; Figure 8 shows band structures for various semiconductors; Figure 9 shows GaAs with spin-orbit coupling; Figure 10/11 evaluate LRI errors and pseudopotential sensitivity in MgO and GaAs.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the scientific need, details the mathematical derivation of the G0W0 framework in the NAO-PP representation, describes specific algorithmic optimizations (LRI compression, q-point treatment, SOC), presents benchmarks against VASP and FHI-aims, and concludes with a discussion on pseudopotential selection strategies.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
